behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because the failure boundary is the handoff between history management and the LLM request builder (wrong slice/order can happen across modules even if each function is locally correct). Integration beats System because no real OS/network/subprocess behavior is required; an in-process LLM client spy can expose the exact bug. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text focused on input trimming ('history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages'), not model output quality or multi-agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire chat-orchestration module + history store adapter + LLM client stub. Feed >10 prior messages, invoke reply generation, and assert the outbound LLM payload contains exactly the latest 10 messages in correct chronological order and role labels.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because relevance depends on prompt assembly + context wiring, not a single pure function. System beats Integration because mocked model responses would hide real off-topic behavior seen only with real model calls. Agentic beats System because correctness depends on model judgment quality (on-topic vs generic), which is non-deterministic and cannot be validated by transport-level/system checks alone. Rejection gate: Workflow is disqualified because this behavior is a single generation-quality decision, not a multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run a curated question set through the real configured LLM path, score relevance against expected intent/rubric, and fail when responses are generic/off-topic beyond threshold. Keep retries/temperature policy identical to production to surface true model behavior.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because this crosses tone-check logic and regeneration orchestration boundaries. Agentic beats Integration because 'correctly identifies' tone suitability is model-output-quality dependent; stubs can force pass/fail and hide real misclassification. Rejection gate: Workflow is disqualified by behavior scope: it requires correct tone flagging plus regeneration trigger, but not validation of a full multi-step agent pipeline outcome across multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use real tone-check path on representative overly formal drafts for casual-tier customers, assert the check flags them and regeneration is invoked. Validate over multiple runs/prompts to detect unstable or systematically wrong tone judgments.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because persistence correctness is at service→repository→schema/clock boundaries. Integration beats System because a local real test DB (or transactional embedded DB) is sufficient to catch missing rows, wrong linkage, and timestamp ordering without OS/network/subprocess dependencies. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text focused on persistence ('persisted to the database with correct timestamps'), not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute conversation handling with deterministic LLM/tone-check stubs that produce one initial response plus regeneration path, then assert DB records include user message, rejected draft, final response, and monotonically correct timestamps with proper conversation foreign keys.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because this is state handoff/orchestration across generation, tone-check outcome, and response-delivery modules. Integration beats System because no real OS/subprocess/network behavior is necessary to expose draft-leak bugs; in-process wiring with controlled doubles can reproduce them. Rejection gate: Agentic is disqualified by explicit precondition text 'When the tone check triggers regeneration' (the model decision is assumed already made); the behavior under test is deterministic replacement/visibility logic. Workflow is disqualified because it does not require validating multiple real agentic decisions in sequence, only post-trigger state replacement.
behaviors.test_description: Force the regeneration path with controlled doubles (first draft rejected, second accepted), run through the real orchestration and delivery layer, and assert all customer-facing outputs/channels contain only the second response while the first remains non-visible (optionally audit-only).
